aquaSettings.pageDescription: Manage basic behaviors in the Aqua module from a single screen.

form.title: Aqua Settings
form.description: Manage transfer and project behavior from a single screen.

fields.partialTransferOccupiedCageMode: Partial Transfer to Full Cage Rule

requireFullTransfer.true: In transfer operations, it is mandatory to transport all fish in the source.
requireFullTransfer.false: A certain amount of transportation is allowed in transfer transactions.

allowProjectMerge.false: The project merging process is kept closed.

partialTransferOccupiedCageMode.0: In partial transfer, if the target cage is full, the process is blocked.
partialTransferOccupiedCageMode.1: In partial transfer, if the target cage is full, it is only allowed if the same batch is available.
partialTransferOccupiedCageMode.2: In partial transfer, the operation is allowed even if the target cage is full.
partialTransferOccupiedCageMode.disabledByFullTransfer: This rule is currently disabled since the transfer is completely mandatory.

feedCostFallbackStrategy.0: If the charge cannot be attributed to the actual goods acceptance cost, the weighted average cost is used.
feedCostFallbackStrategy.1: If the feeding cannot be attributed to the actual goods acceptance cost, FIFO cost logic is used.
feedCostFallbackStrategy.2: If the recharge cannot be attributed to the actual goods acceptance cost, the last purchase price is used.

projectDetailReport.readGuideTitle: How Do I Read the Report?
projectDetailReport.readGuideDescription: The cards show the project total, the lower drop-down rows show cage-based daily transactions.
projectDetailReport.readGuideCurrentFish: Available Fish: The number of live fish from the beginning, less fish waste and shipments.
projectDetailReport.readGuideShipment: Shipment Quantity: on the left is the quantity, on the right is the total of grams of biomass.
projectDetailReport.readGuideDelta: Delta columns show net intraday change (+/-).

projectDetailReport.consistencyFormula: Consistency check: Start - Wastage - Shipment = Available

rawKpiReport.description: Shows operational and biological raw KPI areas by project and cage.
rawKpiReport.loadFailed: Failed to load raw KPI data.
rawKpiReport.tableDescription: Biological performance and operational outcomes are listed per active cage.
rawKpiReport.dictionaryDescription: You can see what each field says and how it is calculated in this section.

descriptions.survivalPct: This area shows how much of the fish originally placed in the cage is still alive. When calculating, the starting number of fish and the number of existing live fish are used; It is read from the remaining population after transfer and shipment.
descriptions.mortalityPct: This field shows the accumulated total wastage relative to the starting stock. The total number of dead fish in wastage records is calculated by dividing by the starting number of fish.
descriptions.fcr: It is the Feed Conversion Ratio field. The total amount of feed recorded in the system is divided by the resulting net biomass increase. As the value decreases, it is understood that less feed is used for the same growth.
descriptions.adgGramPerDay: Average Daily Gain field. The difference between the initial average grams of fish and the current average grams is divided by the number of days at sea. Returns the daily average growth per fish.
descriptions.sgrPctPerDay: It is the Specific Growth Rate field. The natural logarithm of the initial and current gram values ​​is taken and the difference is reduced to a daily basis. It is especially meaningful for comparing groups at different weight levels.
descriptions.densityPct: It is the temporary usage rate for cage density. Since there is currently no actual volume or m3 model, the available biomass is divided by the defined capacity biomass of the cage. So this field should be read as the capacity occupancy percentage for now.
descriptions.forecastBiomassKg30d: It shows the rough biomass expected to be formed after 30 days based on the final growth rate. The current biomass is taken, the estimated daily biomass increase is added and carried over to the 30-day window.

formulas.survivalPct: Survival % = current live fish / starting fish * 100
formulas.adgGramPerDay: ADG = (current average grams - initial average grams) / days at sea
formulas.densityPct: Density % = available biomass / cage capacity biomass * 100
formulas.forecastBiomassKg30d: Estimated biomass = current biomass + daily biomass increase * {{forecastDays}} days

businessKpiReport.description: Shows harvest readiness, forecast confidence and hypothetical revenue/margin areas by project and cage.
businessKpiReport.loadFailed: Business KPI data could not be loaded.
businessKpiReport.tableDescription: Decision support metrics and hypothetical economic indicators are listed by active mesh.
businessKpiReport.dictionaryDescription: These fields are derived from existing operations data and business commentary rules. If there are priced goods acceptance and shipment lines, the system uses them; Areas without price data are not forcefully estimated.
businessKpiReport.assumptionDescription: The feed cost is derived from the goods receipt lines and the sales price is derived from the shipment lines. If there is no suitable price history, the system only accepts the missing parts as 0 and does not produce imaginary figures.

descriptions.estimatedFeedCost: The total feed quantity is found by multiplying the feed cost per kg derived from the goods acceptance history. If there is no price history for the relevant stock, only the missing part is considered 0.
descriptions.feedCostPerCurrentKg: The found feed cost is divided by the available live biomass. Thus, it answers the question at what cost level the project carries kg today.
descriptions.projectedHarvestBiomassKg: The growth and biomass trend in raw KPI is carried over to the business layer. If the current growth pace is maintained, the expected harvest biomass at the end of the forecast period is shown.
descriptions.projectedRevenue: The estimated harvest biomass is multiplied by the selling price per kg derived from shipping history. If there is no sales history, the system calculates the turnover cautiously, increasing it as data arrives.
descriptions.projectedGrossMargin: Feed cost is deducted from the estimated turnover. The system does not forcefully predict price areas for which there is no data; Therefore, the margin calculation is as strong as the current real price coverage.
descriptions.daysToTarget: Take the difference between the current average grams and the target harvest grams and divide by ADG. If ADG is not present or the growth rate is zero, the area cannot be calculated.
descriptions.harvestReadinessPct: This field is not a single financial data, but a decision support score. Weight progression, survival performance, and FCR quality combine to produce a preparation score between 0-100.
descriptions.forecastConfidencePct: It shows how reliable the forecast can be. If there is a recent weigh-in, regular feeding has been done in recent days, and the data set is mature enough, the score increases.

formulas.estimatedFeedCost: Feed cost = total feed (kg) * feed cost per kg. The cost is derived from the goods acceptance history and the fallback method.
formulas.feedCostPerCurrentKg: Cost per live kg = estimated feed cost / available biomass (kg)
formulas.projectedHarvestBiomassKg: Estimated harvest biomass = current biomass + daily biomass increase * {{forecastDays}} days
formulas.projectedRevenue: Estimated turnover = estimated harvested biomass * selling price. The sales price is derived from shipping history.
formulas.projectedGrossMargin: Estimated gross margin = estimated turnover - feed cost. The account is only as strong as its current true price coverage.
formulas.daysToTarget: Days remaining = (target grams {{targetHarvestGram}} - current grams) / ADG
formulas.harvestReadinessPct: Preparation % = weight progress score * 0.55 + survival * 0.25 + FCR score * 0.20
formulas.forecastConfidencePct: Forecast confidence = weighing closeness + feeding consistency + data maturity score

openingImport.title: Initial Load / Data Migration
openingImport.description: Bring active projects, cages, and opening stock from the legacy system into Aqua in a controlled way. Download the Excel template, fill it in, map the columns, preview the data, and import it in one step when it is clean.
openingImport.cutoverModeHint: If you only want to start with today's net stock, Opening Stock is enough. If you also want to bring summarized historic goods receipt, wastage, feeding, and shipment data, fill in the related opening sheets as well. If Opening Stock is empty, the system derives the net opening balance from the goods receipt and wastage summaries.

guide.title: What are the opening templates for?
guide.description: To make setup clearer, each opening sheet is explained by business purpose. The same guide is included as the first sheet in the Excel template.

openingStock.title: Opening Stock
openingStock.body: The net fish count and average gram you have on the day you start using Aqua. If historical document detail is not available, this sheet is enough to start live stock.

openingGoodsReceipts.title: Opening Goods Receipt
openingGoodsReceipts.body: Carries fish entries that happened before Aqua as summary documents. Fill it when the stock source and historical entry trail should appear in reports.

openingShipments.title: Opening Shipment
openingShipments.body: Carries fish that were sold or shipped before Aqua went live. If a sales price is entered, revenue, margin, and Business KPI include those historical sales.

cageWarehouse.title: Cage-Warehouse Mapping
cageWarehouse.body: WarehouseCode in the Cages sheet is optional. If filled, the cage is actively mapped to that ERP/Netsis warehouse; a cage name like B2 is no longer assumed to be a warehouse.

openingMortalityFeedings.title: Opening Wastage and Feeding
openingMortalityFeedings.body: Fill these if historical wastage and feed consumption should appear in FCR, survival rate, and production reports. They can be left empty if details are unknown.

recommendedPath.title: Simplest Recommended Path
recommendedPath.body: For a live-stock-only start, Projects, Cages, and OpeningStock are enough. Add the related summary sheets only when historical movement analysis is needed.

dateFormat.title: Date Format
dateFormat.body: The recommended format for all date fields is 2026-01-31. Turkish format such as 31.01.2026 and Excel serial dates are also supported.

0.title: Projects
0.when: Main information for the project that will be opened in Aqua. Project code and project name must be clear in every initial load file.
0.rule: ProjectCode must be unique. If the same project was already created, the import is blocked and the user is warned.

1.title: Cages
1.when: Defines cages linked to the project. A cage can be named B2, but that name is no longer automatically treated as a Netsis warehouse.
1.rule: WarehouseCode is optional. If filled, the cage is actively mapped to the related ERP/Netsis warehouse.

2.title: Opening Stock
2.when: Used to enter the net live stock and biomass available on the day you start using Aqua.
2.rule: If historical details are unknown, this is the safest starting point. It does not create historical documents; it creates opening balance.

3.title: Opening Goods Receipt
3.when: Used to carry fish entries made before Aqua as summary documents.
3.rule: Fill it when the stock source and historical entry movement should appear in reports.

4.title: Opening Shipment
4.when: Used to carry fish that were sold or shipped before Aqua went live as summary shipments.
4.rule: For Business KPI, revenue/margin, and historical sales count/kg tracking, fill UnitPrice if available; otherwise it is treated as 0.

5.title: Opening Wastage and Feeding
5.when: Used when historical wastage and feed consumption should appear in reports and FCR/survival rate should be meaningful from day one.
5.rule: Can be left empty if details are unknown. If filled, batch, stock, and cage information must match.

6.title: Date Format
6.when: Applies to start, assignment, balance, goods receipt, wastage, feeding, and shipment dates.
6.rule: Recommended format is yyyy-MM-dd, for example 2026-01-31. Turkish format such as 31.01.2026 and Excel serial dates can also be used.

openingImport.summaryDescription: Row-level validation results appear here after preview.
openingImport.mappingDescription: Match Excel columns to system fields. Required fields must not be left empty.
openingImport.sheetMissing: This sheet was not found in the file or it is empty.

cleanupSoftDeleted.title: Deleted test records found
cleanupSoftDeleted.description: These codes were created and deleted during testing. If you approve, the system will try to permanently clean the deleted project/cage records shown in this preview; if related movements exist, the operation stops for safety.
cleanupSoftDeleted.confirm: This action only tries to permanently clean the deleted test project/cage records found in this preview. If related movements exist, the operation is stopped. Continue?
cleanupSoftDeleted.success: {{projects}} project and {{cages}} cage test records were cleaned. Please run preview again.
cleanupSoftDeleted.failed: Deleted test records could not be cleaned.

steps.uploadDescription: After downloading and filling in the template, select the Excel file and start the preview.
